benchmark_context.TODO_domain_routing_artifact: TODO Daniel: this hit appears as depression→als in our domain tags. That is a routing artifact, not a biological claim. Reasons: (1) ketamine's strongest A→B literature in our corpus comes from depression studies (treatment-resistant depression is the most-published ketamine use case in recent years), (2) NMDA receptor → pain signaling is co-indexed with ALS literature because excitotoxicity is the dominant B→C extraction signal for NMDA in our current 8-domain corpus, (3) pain itself is not one of the 8 Robertium domains, so the outcome got tagged with its nearest neighbor in extraction (ALS). The biology (ketamine modulates NMDA which is implicated in pain) is real; the domain labels just reflect what is and isn't in our current corpus.
